name: Beacon
description_short: Beacon is a local government GIS (Geographic Information System) platform providing web-based access to property records, assessor data, and public records across multiple U.S. counties and municipalities.
description_long:
Beacon is a web-based Geographic Information System (GIS) platform operated by Schneider Corporation that provides public access to local government property records and geographic data. The platform aggregates assessor records, property appraisals, and county/municipal records across dozens of counties and cities in over 20 U.S. states including Florida, Georgia, Colorado, Illinois, Indiana, and others.

The service allows users to search and view property information, assessments, and records through an interactive map-based interface. Users can log in to accounts, purchase subscriptions for enhanced access, and browse detailed county and municipal property data organized by state and locality. The platform serves as a centralized portal for accessing public records that would otherwise require visiting individual county assessor offices or navigating separate municipal systems.

Beacon distinguishes itself by offering comprehensive coverage across multiple states and counties through a single unified platform, making property record research more convenient than visiting individual county assessor websites. The GIS-based interface provides geographic context and mapping tools that standard text-based record searches do not offer. The platform appears designed primarily for real estate professionals, property researchers, government staff, and members of the public conducting property due diligence.

However, Beacon is fundamentally a public records access platform, not a consumer finance product. It provides no lending, credit services, financial products, or payday alternatives. The current categorization as a "payday-alternative" is factually incorrect—this is a government data aggregation service with no relevance to consumer lending or credit needs.
services: Web-based GIS mapping interface for property records
County assessor record aggregation and search
User account creation and login management
County and municipality record organization and browsing
Geographic mapping tools for property location
pros: Covers property records across 20+ states and dozens of counties through single platform
Provides GIS mapping interface for geographic context alongside property data
Aggregates records from multiple county assessor offices into unified searchable database
Subscription model available for users needing frequent access to records
Includes property appraisal data, assessment information, and public record integration
User account system allows saved searches and personalized access
cons: Not a consumer finance product—provides no lending, credit services, or payday alternatives
Requires subscription purchase for full access beyond basic viewing
Coverage limited to selected counties and municipalities, not nationwide
Platform appears designed for real estate professionals and government staff, not general consumers
No relevance to credit building, emergency cash needs, or financial assistance
best_for: Real estate professionals conducting property due diligence and market research
Property appraisers and assessors accessing multi-county records
Government staff managing local property records and assessments
Researchers and attorneys requiring historical property information
diagnosis: Beacon is a government GIS and public records platform, not a consumer finance or payday alternative service. It should be recategorized from "payday-alternatives" to "atm" or removed entirely from CreditDoc as it falls outside the consumer finance lending and credit services scope of the platform.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
